channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nairobi Water Artisan Faces Charges Over Fake KCSE Certificate and Salary Fraud
item.description: The Director of Public Prosecutions (DPP) has arraigned Joshua Attuah Omia, an artisan at Nairobi City Water and Sewerage Company, over allegations of using a forged Kenya Certificate of Secondary Edu...

item.title: President Marcos clarifies no government action taken to arrest Senator Dela Rosa after Senate gunshots
item.description: On May 13, 2026, gunshots were reported in the Philippine Senate, causing a temporary lockdown of the premises. President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. issued a recorded statement clarifying that neither he no...

item.title: Starmer Reaffirms Confidence in Streeting Amid Labour Leadership Tensions
item.description: During the State Opening of Parliament, Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer faced mounting pressure over the stability of his Labour government. Health Secretary Wes Streeting reportedly confronted Starme...

item.title: The Dachau Bell: A Symbol of Hope and Freedom in 1945
item.description: During the final days of World War II, prisoners at Dachau concentration camp created a small bell from scrap metal and wire, which became a poignant symbol of hope and resistance amid the camp's extr...

item.title: Lahore experiences heavy rain and hail, causing waterlogging in low-lying areas
item.description: Lahore faced heavy rain and hailstorms on Wednesday, resulting in flooding of low-lying areas and traffic disruptions on major roads. The Pakistan Meteorological Department had earlier predicted rain ...

item.title: New Brunswick Introduces Bill to Streamline Mining Project Approvals
item.description: The government of New Brunswick has introduced new legislation aimed at modernizing and streamlining the province's mining sector. The bill is designed to replace the outdated Mining Act of 1985, with...

item.title: Gunmen Attack Customs Checkpoints, Two Officers Feared Dead in Kebbi
item.description: For early hours of Wednesday, gunmen wey people dey suspect say na Lakurawa gang, attack two Customs checkpoints for Tuga community, Bagudu Local Government Area, Kebbi State. According to source wey ...

item.title: A Retrospective Look at Sandra Dee’s Career and Iconic Roles in the 1960s
item.description: Sandra Dee, born Alexandra Zuck in 1942, was an American actress renowned for her portrayal of the quintessential 'girl-next-door' ingénue. Starting as a child model, she transitioned to film in her t...

item.title: FCT High Court Continues Forgery Trial, Restrains Lawyer from Public Statements
item.description: The High Court of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT) in Apo on Wednesday reject all efforts by Abuja-based lawyer Victor Giwa to stop the ongoing trial against him for forgery and impersonation. Just...

item.title: Internal Records Reveal Fraud and Security Concerns at Canadian Visa Centres Abroad
item.description: Internal documents obtained by CBC News have highlighted ongoing fraud and security issues at Canadian visa application centres operated by VFS Global in countries like Bangladesh, Russia, and Mexico....

item.title: Shirley Temple: From Child Star to Diplomat and Cultural Icon
item.description: Shirley Temple (1928–2014) was one of Hollywood’s most celebrated child stars and later became a pioneering diplomat. Known for her 56 signature curls, Shirley’s mother meticulously styled her hair fo...

item.title: Renovations at State House Nairobi Trigger Public Debate on Design Changes
item.description: State House Nairobi has been making waves on social media after President William Ruto carried out renovations at the official residence. The historic building, once known for its colonial red-tiled r...

item.title: Cambridge postpones AS-level Mathematics exam after paper leak
item.description: Cambridge International Education (CIE) in Pakistan has confirmed that the AS-level Mathematics paper 52 (9709), held on May 12, was leaked ahead of the scheduled exam. Students reported that the pape...

item.title: Welsh Labour Faces Strategic Reckoning After Historic Election Defeat
item.description: The May 7 election marked an unprecedented defeat for Welsh Labour, ending over a century of dominance in the Senedd and leaving the party in opposition with only nine seats. The collapse is not merel...

item.title: White House Aides Report Friction Over Natalie Harp's Role in Managing Trump's Late-Night Truth Social Posts
item.description: According to a Wall Street Journal analysis, President Donald Trump has posted more than 8,800 times on Truth Social since returning to the White House in January 2025. This includes dozens of late-ni...

item.title: PM Modi urges citizens to prioritise wise spending rather than cutting expenses
item.description: According to reliable sources, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has emphasised that his recent appeal is not intended to reduce overall spending, but rather to encourage citizens to spend more judiciously...

item.title: 26-year-old man dies following collision with pedestrians in Arnold
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Market Place, Arnold, Nottinghamshire, in the early hours of Saturday when a Vauxhall Astra struck a group of pedestrians. Among the victims was Tyler Fairman, aged 26, w...

item.title: Thousands Affected by Floods in Khayelitsha as Emergency Relief Continues
item.description: Heavy rains have left over 5,000 homes in the Island informal settlement in Khayelitsha, Cape Town, still flooded, affecting thousands of residents. Community members have opened their homes to displa...

item.title: Quintessenz Revitalizes a 400-Year-Old Greek Ruin with Colorful Tapestries
item.description: In the small town of Kagatika on Paxos island, Greece, a 400-year-old ruin that once served as a factory has been transformed by an artistic installation created by the design duo Quintessenz. The ins...

item.title: Over 1,000 passengers remain on cruise ship amid gastrointestinal illness outbreak
item.description: Over 1,000 passengers are currently being held on the UK-operated cruise ship Ambition, docked in Bordeaux, France, following an outbreak of gastrointestinal illness that has affected 49 individuals. ...
